Type: efiL4zaggiN (Niggaz4Life spelled backwards) is the second and final studio album by N.W.A., and the only N.W.A. album to not feature Ice Cube as a group member. The project is notable for its extremely dark sound, lyrics and subject matter such as rape and mutilation, teetering on horrorcore at some points. This is in stark contrast to other N.W.A. projects which are somewhat lighter in tone.

Name:
Helter Skelter
(Collaboration with Ice Cube)
(Heltah Skeltah)
Notes:
(12/15/1992) (The Chronic officially releases)
(04/XX/1995) (The D.O.C. and Erotic D start working on his version of Helter Skelter)
(01/23/1996) (The D.O.C. releases his version of Helter Skelter)
Type: Helter Skelter was a scrapped collab album between Dr. Dre and Ice Cube, and was slated to release as the next Death Row Records album following Snoop Dogg's "Doggystyle" album. According to The D.O.C., Dre wanted to base the album off of post-apocalyptic themes, and asked The D.O.C. to read various novels on the apocalypse whilst writing verses on the album for him. Although the exact reason for scrapping the project is unknown, the final nail in the coffin was The D.O.C. leaving Death Row Records due to a dispute with Dre and releasing tracks intended for Helter Skelter on his own album of the same name in January 1996. The album's lead single, Natural Born Killaz, was released on the Murder Was The Case soundtrack in 1994.

Name: Detox [V1]
Notes:
(11/16/1999) (2001 officially releases)
(02/??/2002) (Detox sessions start in full force)
(08/??/2004) (Dre gives up on Detox)
(11/??/2004) (Dre announces that he's back to working on Detox)
Type: The first era of Detox. Off the heels from 2001 Dre took some time to help bring up Eminem and Xzibit's albums and work with other artists, of course while working on his then supposed to be third album, Detox. Dre himself would state that Detox would be a "hip hop musical" of sorts, conceptualized about a hitman. However, the concept was scrapped a couple years after. Not much material has leaked from this era but at the time Dre was promoting this as his final album before retiring (even claiming that the verse he recorded for Where I'm From would be his last verse ever). In 2004, Dre also mentioned he wanted to put out up to 12 singles for the album as well, however later that year he seems to have restarted development once again. Songs such as Want Me Back would leave a legacy for Dre.

Type: Detox V2 was quite the busy time for Dre and was the start of the many delays that would happen to Detox. During this time Dre would start to branch out for working with artists and would change up the Detox sound. This was also the era when his working relationship with JAY-Z really came to a start. We dont have many leaks from this time but from what we know this was a promising iteration of Detox.

Detox [V3]
Notes:
(09/??/2007) (Dr. Dre flies out to Detroit to help Eminem finish his mixtape)
(03/16/2009) (Eminem, Dr. Dre & 50 Cent fly to Vegas to shoot the We Made You music video)
Type: One of the more lesser heard from eras of Detox is the third iteration, it was mostly carried by Bishop Lamont & Hayes with Slim The Mobster only getting involved near the end of this version around 2007. Dre was noticeably quiet on the released music side with not much content involving his name coming out. This version of Detox would also start to introduce different producers into the sound such as Jake One and DJ Khalil along with working with other artists such as T.I. & Rain 910. Lastly, this era would also introduce Sly Pyper, Dre's most underrated Aftermath artist.

Name: Detox [V4]
Notes:
(03/16/2009) (Eminem, Dr. Dre & 50 Cent fly to Vegas to shoot the We Made You music video)
(11/14/2011) (Dre announces a break from solo music)
Type: The most wanted version of Detox. Coming out of "Relapse" sessions "Detox" is finally put into full focus by Dr. Dre, and the most extensive sessions are held through this period. Singles are released and the album comes the closest it ever did to releasing, but ultimately burns out and Dre steps back from working on his own music again in 2011.

Name: Detox [V6]
Notes:
(??/??/2016) (Compton Deluxe is scrapped)
(03/19/2020) (California is placed under lockdown due to COVID-19, ushering in a new era of Detox)
Type: Following the release of Compton, Dr. Dre enters a semi-retirement from solo music, mainly focusing on supporting new Aftermath signees and only sparatically working on music himself. However, Detox was still being made (being brought back after Compton released), but wasn't the main focus. In 2018, ESPN reporter Chris Haynes asked Dr. Dre is Detox would be coming out, and Dre's response to him was "I'm working on a couple songs right now. We'll see", meaning the title was still likely in use.

JESUS IS KING: The Dr. Dre Version
(Collaboration with Kanye West)
(JESUS IS KING II, JESUS IS LORD)
Notes:
(10/25/2019) (JESUS IS KING officially releases)
(09/24/2020) (Dem Jointz reveals that Kanye is no longer focused on the project)
Type: The release of JESUS IS KING was met with mixed reviews from fans and critics. Kanye then took to Twitter to announce that he was working on a new album with Dr. Dre. Initially conceived as a remix album, it eventually grew to incorporate mainly unreleased material. During the #WESTDAYEVER campaign, Kanye did on Twitter in 2020, it was supposed to release officially, but never did. It ended up being scrapped sometime in 2020, as stated by producer Dem Jointz. Kanye posted the cover in 2022, which is assumed to be for the album.
